mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through increased household income and reduced unemployment risk.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159626 thousand as of July 2014 indicates strong labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 3.88% combined with core inflation at 1.6% (implied from CPIAUCSL) indicates real rates remain accommodative. A sustained period where FEDFUNDS stays below 4.0% signals continued monetary support that reduces equity risk premiums and produces positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 4.0% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during accommodation periods show no statistical difference from baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST at 1,307 thousand annualized rate indicate sustained residential construction demand. A 3-month moving average of HOUST above 1,200 thousand signals strong housing cycle momentum that drives construction employment, materials demand, and consumer wealth effects, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average exceeds 1,200 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,307 thousand as of June 2014
regime_hint: housing recovery phase in post-crisis expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when HOUST trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) at 101.43 reflects manufacturing capacity utilization. A month-over-month decline in INDPRO of more than 0.5% signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with potential supply constraints
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances when INDPRO decline trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly measure consumer and housing demand. When both RSAFS growth and HOUST growth are positive month-over-month simultaneously, this signals broad-based demand strength that supports equity valuations. The interaction captures demand breadth that single-series signals miss, producing positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 733,258 million and HOUST at 1,307 thousand as of June-July 2014
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of instances when both demand proxies are simultaneously positive.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate at 4.4% combined with payroll growth at 159,626 thousand indicates a tightening labor market. When UNRATE falls below 4.5% while PAYEMS continues to grow, this signals wage pressure and potential inflation acceleration that may trigger Fed tightening expectations, producing negative 60-day forward returns as equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.5% while PAYEMS growth remains positive
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% and PAYEMS at 159,626 thousand as of July 2014
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with inflation concerns
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when tight labor market trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 23,770.976 billion chained 2017 dollars combined with inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 324.368 indicates moderate real growth with contained price pressures. When real GDP growth remains above 2% annualized while inflation stays below 2%, this signals sustainable expansion that supports equity valuations, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: Real GDP growth annualized > 2% with inflation year-over-year < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23,770.976 billion and CPIAUCSL at 324.368 as of Q2 2014
regime_hint: goldilocks expansion with moderate growth and contained inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 56% of instances when goldilocks regime trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 101.43 reflects manufacturing health. A sustained 3-month trend of INDPRO growth above 0.3% month-over-month signals strengthening supply-side capacity and productivity that supports profit margins and equity returns. This always-on trend mechanism produces positive 20-day forward returns when the trend is active.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.4279 as of July 2014 indicates stable manufacturing
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply-side momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 53% of periods when INDPRO shows positive trend.
